S-MD.B.7.reasoning: The standard involves analyzing decisions and strategies using probability concepts. The ontology lacks decision analysis and probability concepts.

M.reasoning: The ontology has 'SpatialModelling' but lacks a high-level, general concept for 'MathematicalModeling' which applies to real-world problem-solving across algebra, geometry, and statistics.

tasks.cluster_description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for 7.SP.C

Extend ontology to support: 7.SP.C.5 (Add Probability Theory Concepts), 7.SP.C.6 (Add Empirical Probability and Relative Frequency), 7.SP.C.7a (Add Probability Models and Uniform Probability), 7.SP.C.7b (Add Probability and Probability Modeling), 7.SP.C.8a (Add Compound Events and Sample Spaces), 7.SP.C.8b (Add Probability Representations), 7.SP.C.8c (Add Simulation and Frequency Generation). Details:
- 7.SP.C.5: Create a new branch for Probability under the main mathematics class, including concepts like Probability of an Event, Likelihood, and the Probability Scale (0 to 1).
- 7.SP.C.6: Introduce Area classes for Relative Frequency, Empirical Probability, and Probability Approximation under the Probability branch.
- 7.SP.C.7a: Introduce Area classes for Probability Models, Uniform Probability, and Theoretical Probability under the Probability branch.
- 7.SP.C.7b: Introduce a new Area for Probability and ProbabilityModel, along with related concepts like ChanceProcess and FrequencyObservation.
- 7.SP.C.8a: Create new Areas for CompoundEvents and SampleSpace to support middle school probability standards.
- 7.SP.C.8b: Add TreeDiagram, OrganizedList, and ProbabilityTable as representational scopes or areas under a new Probability branch.
- 7.SP.C.8c: Introduce Simulation and FrequencyGeneration as new Areas to support experimental probability.

tasks.cluster_description: Perform operations on matrices and use matrices in applications.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for N-VM.C
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: N-VM.C.6 (Add Matrix Concept and Applications), N-VM.C.7 (Add Matrix Arithmetic and Scalar Multiplication), N-VM.C.8 (Add Matrix Operations), N-VM.C.9 (Add Matrix Multiplication Properties), N-VM.C.10 (Add Special Matrices and Determinants), N-VM.C.11 (Add Matrix-Vector Multiplication and Linear Transformations), N-VM.C.12 (Add Matrix Transformations of the Plane). Details:
- N-VM.C.6: Introduce a 'MatrixConcept' Area to represent rectangular arrays of numbers, along with scopes or areas for data representation and network incidence relationships.
- N-VM.C.7: Add 'MatrixArithmetic' and 'MatrixScalarMultiplication' as Areas to support operations on matrices.
- N-VM.C.8: Add 'MatrixAddition', 'MatrixSubtraction', and 'MatrixMultiplication' as Areas under a new 'MatrixArithmetic' branch.
- N-VM.C.9: Add 'MatrixMultiplication' Area and link it to algebraic laws like 'CommutativeLaw', 'AssociativeLaw', and 'DistributiveLaw' to represent non-commutative properties.
- N-VM.C.10: Add 'Determinant', 'IdentityMatrix', 'ZeroMatrix', and 'MatrixInverse' as Areas to support advanced matrix properties and inverse calculations.
- N-VM.C.11: Add 'MatrixVectorMultiplication' and 'LinearTransformation' Areas to represent matrices acting on vectors.
- N-VM.C.12: Add 'MatrixTransformation' and 'DeterminantAreaRelation' Areas to represent 2x2 matrix transformations on the Cartesian plane and their geometric interpretations.

tasks.cluster_description: Solve systems of equations.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for A-REI.C
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: A-REI.C.5 (Add Systems of Equations and Algebraic Proofs), A-REI.C.6 (Add Systems of Linear Equations), A-REI.C.7 (Add Non-Linear Systems of Equations), A-REI.C.8 (Add Linear Algebra, Matrices, and Vectors), A-REI.C.9 (Add Matrix Operations and Inverses). Details:
- A-REI.C.5: Create Area individuals for SystemsOfEquations, LinearSystems, and AlgebraicProofs to support systems of equations standards.

- A-REI.C.7: Introduce Area terms for NonLinearSystems, CircleEquations, and intersection of curves.
- A-REI.C.8: Add Area terms for LinearAlgebra, MatrixRepresentation, and VectorEquations to support matrix-based systems of equations.
- A-REI.C.9: Add Area terms for MatrixOperations, MatrixInverse, and TechnologyAidedComputation.

tasks.cluster_description: Understand the concept of a function and use function notation.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for F-IF.A
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: F-IF.A.1 (Add Core Function Concepts), F-IF.A.2 (Add Function Notation and Evaluation), F-IF.A.3 (Add Sequences and Recursive Functions). Details:
- F-IF.A.1: Introduce new Area individuals for FunctionConcept, DomainAndRange, and FunctionGraphing to represent the definition, mapping, and graphical representation of mathematical functions.
- F-IF.A.2: Introduce new Area individuals for FunctionNotation and FunctionEvaluation to represent evaluating functions and interpreting function notation in context.
- F-IF.A.3: Introduce new Area individuals for MathematicalSequences, RecursiveFunctions, and ArithmeticGeometricSequences to represent sequences as functions defined over integer domains.

tasks.description:

- F-IF.B.4: Introduce new Area individuals for FunctionKeyFeatures, GraphInterpretation, and ExtremaAndIntercepts to represent identifying intercepts, extrema, intervals of increase/decrease, and periodicity of functions.
- F-IF.B.5: Introduce new Area individuals for DomainSpecification and QuantitativeRelationships to represent relating a function's domain to its graph and real-world context.
- F-IF.B.6: Introduce a new Area individual for AverageRateOfChange to represent calculating and estimating the rate of change of a function over an interval.

tasks.cluster_description: Analyze functions using different representations.

tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: F-IF.C.7a (Add Quadratic Functions and Graphing), F-IF.C.7b (Add Piecewise and Radical Functions), F-IF.C.7c (Add Polynomial Functions and Zeros). Details:
- F-IF.C.7a: Introduce new Area individuals for QuadraticFunctions and QuadraticGraphing to represent graphing linear and quadratic functions with their key features.
- F-IF.C.7b: Introduce new Area individuals for PiecewiseFunctions, AbsoluteValueFunctions, and RadicalFunctions to represent graphing non-linear and piecewise-defined functions.
- F-IF.C.7c: Introduce new Area individuals for PolynomialFunctions and PolynomialZeros to represent graphing polynomial functions and identifying their zeros and end behavior.

Extend ontology to support: S-CP.B.9 (Add Combinatorics and Probability Concepts). Details:
- S-CP.B.9: Introduce a new Combinatorics area with Permutations and Combinations classes, and a Probability area with Compound Events.

tasks.cluster_description: Use the rules of probability to compute probabilities of compound events in a uniform probability model.
tasks.title: Generate Dataset for S-CP.B
tasks.description:
Implement/extend generators and views to cover: S-CP.B.6, S-CP.B.7, S-CP.B.8. Suggested tasks:
- S-CP.B.6: Add a specific calculation concept for conditional probability based on sample space subsets.
- S-CP.B.7: Add the Addition Rule of Probability as a specific mathematical law under Statistics/Probability.
- S-CP.B.8: Add the Multiplication Rule of Probability as a specific mathematical law under Statistics/Probability.

tasks.cluster_description: Calculate expected values and use them to solve problems.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for S-MD.A
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: S-MD.A.1 (Add Random Variables and Probability Distributions), S-MD.A.2 (Add Expected Value and Probability Distribution), S-MD.A.3 (Add Theoretical Probability and Probability Distributions), S-MD.A.4 (Add Empirical Probability and Probability Distributions). Details:
- S-MD.A.1: Create classes for Random Variables, Sample Spaces, and Probability Distributions under a new Probability area.
- S-MD.A.2: Introduce Expected Value and Probability Distribution as Area concepts under Probability and Statistics.
- S-MD.A.3: Incorporate Theoretical Probability and Probability Distribution concepts into the ontology.
- S-MD.A.4: Incorporate Empirical Probability and Probability Distribution concepts into the ontology.

tasks.cluster_description: Use probability to evaluate outcomes of decisions.
tasks.title: Extend Ontology for S-MD.B
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: S-MD.B.5a (Add Expected Payoff and Games of Chance), S-MD.B.5b (Add Strategy Evaluation and Expected Value), S-MD.B.6 (Add Probability-Based Decision Making), S-MD.B.7 (Add Decision Analysis and Probability Concepts). Details:
- S-MD.B.5a: Introduce Expected Payoff and Games of Chance concepts under the Probability area.
- S-MD.B.5b: Introduce Strategy Evaluation and Expected Value concepts to support decision theory standards.
- S-MD.B.6: Introduce concepts for Fair Decisions, Random Number Generators, and Probability-Based Decision Making.
- S-MD.B.7: Introduce Decision Analysis, Strategy Analysis, and Probability Concepts to the ontology.

tasks.title: Extend Ontology for HS
tasks.description:
Extend ontology to support: M (Add Mathematical Modeling Concept). Details:
- M: Add 'MathematicalModeling' as a high-level Area or Ability to represent the process of choosing and using appropriate mathematics and statistics to analyze empirical situations.
